caption: Coverage of the initial and refined semantic applications, with marker-strength counts. This table documents the move from broad lexical coverage to the conservative refined model.
Global refined coverage for each semantic family, including any-marker coverage and weak/name-only rows.
Core semantic families and composite semantic-change indices by period. This is the primary period-level numerical table supporting the global historical argument.
Within-journal changes in core indices and journal-level trajectory labels. The table supports the claim that the transformation is visible within journals while retaining journal-specific dialects.

Direction of change across journals in all-available, long-journal, common-period, and post-1990 panels. The table summarizes the robustness of the major semantic trends under partially balanced conditions.
Families most affected by marker-strength correction. This table documents which families were most dependent on broad weak or name-only markers.
Journal-level distinctiveness from global period profiles. This table supports interpretation of journals as distinct psychoanalytic discourse dialects.
Selected period-level indices with interpretive labels. This table provides a compact bridge from indices to narrative periodization.
